I know the story. It transpired after Charlie 'Lucky' Luciano had to step down as the head of the syndicate due to being imprisoned for 50 years, then eventually exiled from America. That's when the power struggle began. Luciano wanted Costello to take his place. His other two closest mob friends Bugsy Siegal* was a violent hot head and Mayer Lansky was not Sicilian or Italian, so it fell to Castello to take on the role as head of the family.

Genovese was a very dangerous individual who had worked loyally for Luciano, but he was a homicidal maniac, who would kill without hesitation. He felt overlooked and decided that Costello had to go so he could take over.

*Siegal was eventually killed by order of his own mob friends (Luciano and Lansky) for skimming money from the mob funded construction of the Flamingo hotel in Vegas. The first gambling casino hotel built there, still standing today.

*I WAS a lifelong De Niro fan, but now I can't stand the guy. I doubt I'll watch this movie due to that, as much as the story interests me*

Yknow it’s probably one of the better historically accurate movies based on historical accounts that have been documented. I will say the ending accounts given the the perspective of Frank were new to me. That he was the unknown caller that tipped off state police and that Albert Anastasia was more of a protector than the big baddie in this scenario was different definitely added more context. Not the grittiest film but given it is from the perspective of arguably the most gentlemanly boss in mafia history arguably it made sense that it wasn’t so violent in these years. I think if Vito Genovese had a stand alone film and Martin directed it and Joe Pesci or someone else played the role for Vito and the story focused on him and his life it would have been a bit more gritty like some expected given my knowledge that Vito was as they described in the movie are the accounts of him in history. I think De Niro played them both well I forgot it was De Niro after a little bit and hats off to the casting. Cosmo Jarvis cast as The Chin was awesome. Definitely could see Cosmo Jarvis recast as Sonny Corleone in a future Godfather prequel film or mini series. Didn’t see it until I saw his face shaven and the curly hair in this type of movie. Voice might be the only issue. And Michael Rispoli cast as Albert Anastasia was phenomenal. He was the best in the whole film. I literally thought you know who has yet to get a respectable film?! Albert Anastasia and Michael Rispoli cast as him was amazing. Like I thought to myself yknow he didn’t get his honest shake in Sopranos and I’ve seen him in some of his earlier films and his characters always leave me wanting more. Definitely want to see a stand alone with his as the lead cast as Albert Anastasia
Saw the movie today. I thought the movie was historically spot on. Acting was great. I liked it myself maybe 7 out of 10

The use of 'Trouble So Hard' in this mob trailer is deeply problematic and disturbing. This spiritual originates from the pain and resilience of enslaved people, and using it to add 'grit' and emotional pull to a mob story, solely to monetize from that pain, completely disregards its historical significance. To strip this song of its meaning and exploit the suffering of generations for entertainment is a gross act of cultural misappropriation and incredibly insensitive.
